latestRelease.name: V26.6.0-RC1 "Aimless Albatross"
latestRelease.body: ## Major Milestone: Native Disk Installation BoredOS is now **fully installable to disk**. This transition from a RAM-based/live-only environment to a persistent, disk-backed operating system is made possible by several key additions in this release: - **Official System Installer**: Introducing `boredos_install`, a dedicated utility to manage the installation process. - **Advanced Disk Management**: Added kernel-level support for GPT and MBR writing, partition recognition, and disk rescanning. - **Persistent Storage**: Implemented root filesystem pivoting and persistence provisioning, allowing the OS to run directly from physical storage. - **Filesystem Utilities**: New `fdisk` and `mkfs_fat32` tools for partitioning and formatting drives natively. ## I2C & Input Subsystem A significant new subsystem has been added to support modern laptop hardware: - **I2C Subsystem**: Initial implementation of the I2C bus driver framework. - **Intel LPSS Support**: Added driver for Intel Low Power Subsystem (LPSS) I2C controllers. - **Synopsys DesignWare Driver**: Support for DesignWare I2C IP blocks commonly found in modern SoCs. - **ACPI Integration**: Automated enumeration of I2C devices via ACPI; improved AML parsing for I2C resources; added `acpi_i2c` bridge for device discovery. - **I2C-HID Support**: Initial foundation for I2C-based human interface devices. ## Core Kernel & Architecture - **User Programs mapped with User Pages**: Updated the user programs to use User Pages instead of Kernel Pages. - **Disk-Based Root Support**: Updated boot logic to support booting from a disk-based root filesystem. - **Kernel-Level TTY File Descriptors**: Implemented kernel-level support for TTY file descriptors, enabling improved shell operator support. - **ACPI Enhancements**: Implemented ACPI power shutdown support; improved device enumeration and logging. - **Process Management**: Fixed process slot exhaustion in the process lifecycle; prevented infinite loops during process termination. - **Memory Management**: Hardened the slab allocator; fixed paging race conditions. - **Boot Logic**: Added support for command-line flags at boot; optimised kernel console by removing unnecessary frame flipping, resulting in faster boot times. - **Networking**: Improved network stability and resource management. - **Hardware Support**: Improved PS/2 hardware correctness. ## Filesystem & Storage - **FAT32 Enhancements**: Implemented Long File Name (LFN) support; added root volume abstraction. - **Disk Management**: Added GPT and MBR write support; implemented disk rescan; fixed AHCI slot bugs and partition recognition after `fdisk`. - **Root Filesystem Pivoting**: Implemented root filesystem pivoting and persistence provisioning. - **New Utilities**: FAT32 formatting utility (`mkfs_fat32`); `statfs` support for filesystem statistics. - **Procfs**: Added datetime information to `procfs`. ## Shell & CLI Utilities - **Shell Operators**: `bsh` now supports a comprehensive set of operators: - Pipes: `|` - Redirections: `>`, `>>`, `<` - Logic/Control: `&`, `&&`, `||`, `;` - **New & Updated Commands**: - `grep`: Full-featured, with `-r`, `-v`, `-l`, `-w`, and `-x` flag support. - `find`: New utility for locating files. - `du`: Disk usage reporting with `-H` support. - `df`: Filesystem disk space usage reporting. - `lsblk`: Disk listing command. - `time`: Measures process execution time (aliased from `ptime`). - `ps`: Process status utility. - `hexdump`: Hexadecimal dump utility. - `fdisk`: Disk partitioning utility. - `mkfs_fat`: FAT filesystem formatting tool. - `boredos_install`: Official system installer. - `sysfetch`: Updated with uptime, datetime, and CPU information. ## Graphical Environment & Window Manager - **Rendering Optimizations**: Optimized graphics rendering with caching; implemented thread-safe glyph caching. - **Branding & Assets**: New logo, boot splash, and ASCII art; multiple new wallpapers including `orbital.png`, `Drift.png`, `squiggly.png`, `Web-Of-Connectivity.png`, `flowerdark/light.jpg`, `bored bird.png` and `boredshadow.png`. ## Userland SDK & Libc - **Toolchain**: Integrated TCC (Tiny C Compiler) as the native userland compiler; vendored into the repository for a self-hosted build environment. - **Libc Enhancements**: Added disk management syscalls; expanded `stdio` support; standardised string functions for freestanding environments; replaced `sys_yield` with `sleep` for improved idle efficiency; added support for `mman.h`, `ucontext.h`, and improved POSIX I/O. - **SDK**: Updated headers including `assert.h`, `ctype.h`, `errno.h`, `fcntl.h`, `math.h`, `signal.h`, `unistd.h`, and more. ## Build System - **Makefile Improvements**: Modernised initrd structure and asset management; implemented automatic source file detection; standardised object file naming with `libc_` prefix; added platform detection for generic `make run` rules. ## Contributors [BoredDevNL](https://github.com/BoredDevNL) [Lluciocc](https://github.com/Lluciocc) [Mellurboo](https://github.com/Mellurboo) [janevers-sys](https://github.com/janevers-sys) [zeyadhost](https://github.com/zeyadhost) [artemix1508](https://github.com/artemix1508) [qwroffc](https://github.com/qwroffc) [whitehai11](https://github.com/whitehai11) [naplon74](https://github.com/naplon74) [pixelyblah](https://github.com/pixelyblah) [toiletalphamale](https://github.com/toiletalphamale)
